text.title: Spring Boot Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Spring Boot 3 layered architecture. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Beans MUST receive dependencies via constructor injection (`@RequiredArgsConstructor` or hand-written). Field injection (`@Autowired private Foo foo;`) and setter injection are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Constructor injection enables immutability + final fields + easy testing without Spring; field injection hides cycles and makes unit tests reach for reflection.

rule.statement: Request / response DTOs MUST be Java records distinct from JPA entities. Entities MUST NOT cross controller boundaries.
rule.rationale: Sharing entity + DTO leaks Hibernate proxy state + soft-delete columns; records guarantee immutability across the API surface.

rule.statement: DTO ↔ entity mapping MUST use MapStruct via the annotation processor. Hand-coded mappers and `BeanUtils.copyProperties` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Annotation-processor mappers fail at compile when a property goes missing; hand-coded mappers and reflection-based copy drift silently.

rule.statement: Errors MUST be returned as RFC 7807 `ProblemDetail` via a single `@RestControllerAdvice`. Custom JSON error shapes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: One contract lets clients implement a single parser; ad-hoc shapes multiply parsing logic.

rule.statement: `@Transactional` MUST live on `@Service` methods, not on controllers or repositories. Service classes default to `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`; writes override locally.
rule.rationale: Transactions on controllers blur the boundary; transactions on repositories make composition impossible.

rule.statement: Cross-cutting concerns (auth, logging, tracing, error handling) MUST live in filters, interceptors, or `@RestControllerAdvice`. Per-controller `try/catch` duplication for these concerns is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Advice keeps the controller readable + the cross-cutting policy centralised; per-controller duplication drifts.
